# Citations

All sources visited in-session via WebSearch/WebFetch. Each entry records the URL,
access context, and specific data extracted.

## DPI / HiDPI Scaling

[1] CodersLegacy — "Improve Tkinter Resolution"
<https://coderslegacy.com/python/problem-solving/improve-tkinter-resolution/>
Data: `SetProcessDpiAwareness(1)` call before `Tk()`, `GetScaleFactorForDevice(0)` for scale query.

[2] CPython Issue #119459 — "Tk DPI awareness"
<https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/119459>
Data: Tkinter defaults to DPI-unaware; Tk/Tcl 9.0 is scaling-aware natively.

[3] CPython Issue #70885 — "Tk DPI awareness on Windows" (migrated from bugs.python.org #26698)
<https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/70885>
Data: Long-running upstream tracker for Tk DPI awareness.

[4] CPython Issue #77837 — "IDLE DPI awareness" (migrated from bugs.python.org #33656)
<https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/77837>
Data: IDLE-specific DPI awareness work; supersedes [3].

[5] CustomTkinter Wiki — "Scaling"
<https://github.com/TomSchimansky/CustomTkinter/wiki/Scaling>
Data: CustomTkinter uses `SetProcessDpiAwareness(2)` for per-monitor awareness.

[6] Pydata Beer docs — "Introduction to DPI Awareness"
<https://frothy-brew.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dpi/intro_dpi.html>
Data: Three DPI awareness levels (0=unaware, 1=system, 2=per-monitor).

[7] Tcl Wiki — "tk scaling"
<https://wiki.tcl-lang.org/page/tk+scaling>
Data: Scaling factor = pixels per point (1 pt = 1/72 inch). Factor 1.0 = 72 DPI.

[8] Tcl/Tk 9.0 Official Manual — "tk"
<https://www.tcl-lang.org/man/tcl9.0/TkCmd/tk.html>
Data: Official reference for `tk scaling` subcommand.

[9] ArchWiki — "HiDPI"
<https://wiki.archlinux.org/title/HiDPI>
Data: `Xft.dpi` in `~/.Xresources`, `GDK_SCALE`, integer scaling recommendations.

[10] w3tutorials — "Scale Tkinter GUI for 4K on LinuxMint"
<https://www.w3tutorials.net/blog/scaling-of-tkinter-gui-in-4k-3840-2160-resolution/>
Data: Practical guide for Linux HiDPI tkinter scaling.

[11] Fossies — "tk/library/ttk/fonts.tcl"
<https://fossies.org/linux/tk/library/ttk/fonts.tcl>
Data: Tk source — font size specified in pixels on X11, not points. Per-platform font definitions.

[12] The Register — "Tcl/Tk 9 arrives"
<https://www.theregister.com/2024/10/02/tcltk_version_9/>
Data: Tk 9.0 is "scaling-aware for HiDPI displays" with SVG support.

[13] Tcl Wiki — "Changes in Tcl/Tk 9.0"
<https://wiki.tcl-lang.org/page/Changes+in+Tcl/Tk+9.0>
Data: Official Tk 9.0 change log.

## Theming and Native Appearance

[14] TkDocs — "Styles and Themes"
<https://tkdocs.com/tutorial/styles.html>
Data: Built-in themes per platform; Windows: winnative/vista/xpnative/clam/alt/default/classic; Linux: clam/alt/default/classic.

[15] Tcl Wiki — "List of ttk Themes"
<https://wiki.tcl-lang.org/page/List+of+ttk+Themes>
Data: Comprehensive catalog including third-party themes.

[16] CodersLegacy — "Tkinter ttk Themes"
<https://coderslegacy.com/tkinter-ttk-themes/>
Data: Tutorial confirming theme availability per OS.

[17] sv-ttk — PyPI
<https://pypi.org/project/sv-ttk/>
Data: API: `sv_ttk.set_theme("dark")`, `toggle_theme()`, `get_theme()`. Only affects ttk widgets.

[18] Sun Valley ttk theme — GitHub
<https://github.com/rdbende/Sun-Valley-ttk-theme>
Data: Source repo with usage wiki.

[19] ttkthemes — PyPI
<https://pypi.org/project/ttkthemes/>
Data: 30+ themes; recommends adapta/arc/breeze/yaru for cross-platform use.

[20] ttkthemes docs — "Themes"
<https://ttkthemes.readthedocs.io/en/latest/themes.html>
Data: Full theme catalog with pixmap vs Tcl-based classification.

[21] Azure ttk theme — GitHub
<https://github.com/rdbende/Azure-ttk-theme>
Data: Fluent Design theme with light/dark modes, custom widget styles.

[22] Python docs — "tkinter.ttk"
<https://docs.python.org/3/library/tkinter.ttk.html>
Data: Official ttk reference. ttk-exclusive widgets: Combobox, Separator, Sizegrip, Treeview, Notebook, Progressbar.

[23] GeeksforGeeks — "Difference Between Tkinter and ttk"
<https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/python/difference-between-the-widgets-of-tkinter-and-tkinter-ttk-in-python/>
Data: Side-by-side comparison of tk vs ttk widgets.

[24] darkdetect — GitHub
<https://github.com/albertosottile/darkdetect>
Data: OS dark mode detection. API: `theme()`, `isDark()`, `isLight()`, `listener(callback)`.

[25] darkdetect — PyPI
<https://pypi.org/project/darkdetect/>
Data: No external dependencies. Works on Windows 10+, macOS 10.14+, Linux (limited).

<https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/86484>

[32] CPython Issue #32863 — "Missing support for Emojis in tkinter"
<https://bugs.python.org/issue32863>

Data: Noto Color Emoji causes X error in Tcl/Tk scripts on Fedora.

[34] discuss.python.org — "Tkinter and Unicode emoji variation"

Data: Recent discussion of emoji limitations in tkinter.

## Window Management

[35] PythonGUIs — "Customizing Your Tkinter App Windows"
<https://www.pythonguis.com/tutorials/customized-windows-tkinter/>
Data: `overrideredirect(True)` on Windows removes taskbar entry (WS_EX_TOOLWINDOW). ctypes workaround for taskbar button.

[36] Tcl Wiki — "wm overrideredirect"
<https://wiki.tcl-lang.org/page/wm+overrideredirect>
Data: Focus problems on Linux; tkMessageBox appears behind overrideredirect window on Linux.

[37] copyprogramming.com — "Tkinter Linux: View Window in Taskbar Without Title Bar"
<https://copyprogramming.com/howto/tkinter-linux-how-to-view-window-in-windows-task-bar-which-has-no-title-bar>
Data: overrideredirect opts window out of window manager entirely.

[38] Tcl Wiki — "wm attributes"
<https://wiki.tcl-lang.org/page/wm+attributes>
Data: `-topmost` works reliably on Windows/macOS; some Linux WMs may ignore it.

[39] pythontutorials.net — "How to Make a Tkinter Window Jump to the Front"
<https://www.pythontutorials.net/blog/how-to-make-a-tkinter-window-jump-to-the-front/>
Data: Flash-topmost pattern (set True then False). Combine with `focus_force()`.

[40] GitHub — "linuxmint/muffin #691"
<https://github.com/linuxmint/muffin/issues/691>
Data: `focus_force()` and `lift()` blocked by focus-stealing prevention on Cinnamon.

[41] KDE Discuss — "Ideas for better Focus stealing prevention"
<https://discuss.kde.org/t/ideas-for-better-focus-stealing-prevention/32154>
Data: KDE focus-stealing prevention policies.

[42] GitHub — "rust-windowing/winit #1160"
<https://github.com/rust-windowing/winit/issues/1160>
Data: "No way to avoid focus stealing on X11."

[43] Post.Byes — "Tkinter: modal ok in windows, but not in Linux"
<https://post.bytes.com/forum/topic/python/21425-tkinter-modal-ok-in-windows-but-not-in-linux>
Data: Main window can be closed while modal dialog active on Linux.

[44] Tkinter-discuss — "Modal dialog behavior"
<https://tkinter-discuss.python.narkive.com/lX7LOp6k/modal-dialog-behavior>
Data: Window manager can ignore `grab_set()` on X11.

[45] effbot.org (mirror) — "Dialog Windows"
<http://tkinter.programujte.com/tkinter-dialog-windows.htm>
Data: Proper modal sequence: `transient()`, `wait_visibility()`, `grab_set()`, `wait_window()`.

[46] CPython Issue #26340 — "Modal dialog with transient method"
<https://bugs.python.org/issue26340>
Data: Bug where parent window with transient child fails to iconify properly.

[47] discuss.python.org — "Feature request: Wayland support for TKInter"
<https://discuss.python.org/t/feature-request-wayland-support-for-tkinter/75198>
Data: Tkinter has no native Wayland support; feature request opened Dec 2024.

[48] CPython Issue #128204 — "Wayland support for tkinter"
<https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/128204>
Data: Feature request for native Wayland support.

[49] freedesktop.org — "XWayland menu positioning issue #619"
<https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/wayland/weston/-/issues/619>
Data: Window position setting broken under XWayland.

[50] CPython Issue #132430 — "Turtle graphics glitches on WSLg"
<https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/132430>
Data: Rendering corruption on XWayland due to race conditions.

[51] Tcl Wiki — "Dual or Multiple Monitors"
<https://wiki.tcl-lang.org/page/Dual+or+Multiple+Monitors>
Data: `winfo_screenwidth()` returns combined dimensions on Linux/Mac, primary only on Windows.

[52] TkDocs Tutorial — "Windows and Dialogs"
<https://profjahier.github.io/html/NSI/tkinter/doc_tk_allegee/tutorial/windows.html>
Data: `screeninfo` library recommended for multi-monitor detection.

## System Tray

[53] pystray — PyPI
<https://pypi.org/project/pystray/>
Data: Three Linux backends (appindicator, gtk, xorg). Windows: win32. macOS: darwin.

[54] pystray — Usage Documentation
<https://pystray.readthedocs.io/en/latest/usage.html>
Data: AppIndicator supports all features except default action. GTK backend may produce no icon on GNOME without extension.

[55] pystray — Reference Documentation
<https://pystray.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference.html>
Data: Runtime capability detection: `Icon.HAS_MENU`, `Icon.HAS_DEFAULT_ACTION`, `Icon.HAS_NOTIFICATION`.

[56] pystray — FAQ
<https://pystray.readthedocs.io/en/latest/faq.html>
Data: Xorg backend only works in X sessions, not Wayland.

[57] OMG! Ubuntu — "GNOME 3.26 Removes the Legacy System Tray"
<https://www.omgubuntu.co.uk/2017/09/will-you-miss-gnome-legacy-tray>
Data: GNOME 3.26 removed legacy tray. "large majority of apps work perfectly fine without them."

[58] GNOME Shell Extensions — "AppIndicator and KStatusNotifierItem Support"
<https://extensions.gnome.org/extension/615/appindicator-support/>
Data: Shell extension re-enables tray icon support on GNOME.

[59] Ubuntu Wiki — "ApplicationIndicators"
<https://wiki.ubuntu.com/DesktopExperienceTeam/ApplicationIndicators>
Data: libappindicator uses dbusmenu internally; based on KStatusNotifierItem spec.

[60] GitHub — "AyatanaIndicators/libayatana-appindicator"
<https://github.com/AyatanaIndicators/libayatana-appindicator>
Data: Successor to Canonical's libappindicator. Pystray falls back to ayatana automatically.

[61] GitHub — "i3/i3 #2088"
<https://github.com/i3/i3/issues/2088>
Data: Unity/KDE/GNOME deprecated XEmbed in favor of StatusNotifierItem; simple WM status bars only support XEmbed.

[62] sr.ht — "snixembed"
<https://sr.ht/~steef/snixembed/>
Data: Bridge tool: StatusNotifierHost on DBus → XEmbed tray icons via GTK+3.

[63] GTK 3 Docs — "Gtk.StatusIcon"
<https://docs.gtk.org/gtk3/class.StatusIcon.html>
Data: GtkStatusIcon deprecated in GTK 3.14.

[64] ArchWiki — "Desktop notifications"
<https://wiki.archlinux.org/title/Desktop_notifications>
Data: `org.freedesktop.Notifications` DBus interface for notifications.

## Events and Input

[65] TkDocs Tutorial — "Menus"
<https://tkdocs.com/tutorial/menus.html>
Data: `<<ContextMenu>>` virtual event for cross-platform right-click.

[66] Post.Byes — "Right click on Mac with Tkinter"
<https://post.bytes.com/forum/topic/python/563044-right-click-on-mac-with-tkinter>
Data: Button-1=left, Button-2=middle, Button-3=right on Windows/Linux. macOS: Button-2=right.

[67] Tcl Wiki — "mousewheel"
<https://wiki.tcl-lang.org/page/mousewheel>
Data: Windows/macOS send `<MouseWheel>` with delta. Linux/X11: `<Button-4>/<Button-5>`.

[68] DaniWeb — "Using the Mouse Wheel with Tkinter"
<https://www.daniweb.com/programming/software-development/code/217059/using-the-mouse-wheel-with-tkinter-python>
Data: On Windows, MouseWheel goes to focused window, not window under pointer.

[69] TIP 474 — "Treat mouse wheel events uniformly"
<https://core.tcl-lang.org/tips/doc/trunk/tip/474.md>
Data: Tk 8.7 will translate Button-4/5 to MouseWheel events internally.

[70] ActiveState — "Mousewheel binding multiplatform"
<https://code.activestate.com/recipes/578894-mousewheel-binding-to-scrolling-area-tkinter-multi/>
Data: Cross-platform pattern normalizing delta and event.num.

[71] Tcl Wiki — "Modifier Keys"
<https://wiki.tcl-lang.org/page/Modifier+Keys>
Data: Meta vs Alt distinction. Don't use Command/Option except on Mac. Don't use Mod4 (Windows key) cross-platform.

[72] Tcl/Tk bind manual
<https://www.tcl-lang.org/man/tcl8.4/TkCmd/bind.htm>
Data: Modifier key binding syntax reference.

[73] Tcl Wiki — "Primary Transfer vs. the Clipboard"
<https://wiki.tcl-lang.org/page/Primary+Transfer+vs.+the+Clipboard>
Data: Linux/X11 has PRIMARY (select) and CLIPBOARD (Ctrl+C). Windows/macOS have only CLIPBOARD.

[74] CPython Issue #40452 — "Tkinter/IDLE: preserve clipboard on closure"
<https://bugs.python.org/issue40452>
Data: Clipboard data lost when tkinter exits (owned by dead process).

## Packaging and Distribution

[75] PyInstaller 6.19.0 — Usage Documentation
<https://pyinstaller.org/en/stable/usage.html>
Data: `--onefile` vs `--onedir` behavior. `--windowed`/`--noconsole` for GUI subsystem.

[76] AhmedSyntax — "PyInstaller Onefile: The Complete 2025 Guide"
<https://ahmedsyntax.com/pyinstaller-onefile/>
Data: Onefile extraction adds startup latency. Source quality: SEO blog, cross-reference with [75].

[77] PyInstaller — "When Things Go Wrong"
<https://pyinstaller.org/en/stable/when-things-go-wrong.html>
Data: `--hidden-import` for dynamic imports. `--debug=imports` for diagnosis.

[78] krrt7.dev — "Compilation vs Bundling: Nuitka and PyInstaller"
<https://krrt7.dev/en/blog/nuitka-vs-pyinstaller>
Data: Nuitka 2-3x faster startup, 2-4x runtime improvement vs CPython. Source quality: technical blog with benchmarks.

[79] x321.org — "Empirical: pyinstaller vs nuitka vs cx_Freeze"
<https://x321.org/empirical-pyinstaller-vs-nuitka-vs-cx_freeze/>
Data: Nuitka 58 MB, cx_Freeze 79 MB, PyInstaller 94 MB (Linux CLI app).

[80] Fossies — "Nuitka TkinterPlugin.py"
<https://fossies.org/linux/Nuitka/nuitka/plugins/standard/TkinterPlugin.py>
Data: Nuitka `tk-inter` plugin bundles 87 Tk + 835 Tcl data files.

[81] AhmedSyntax — "cx_Freeze vs PyInstaller in 2026"
<https://ahmedsyntax.com/cx-freeze-vs-pyinstaller/>
Data: Startup comparison (~8s cx_Freeze vs ~50s PyInstaller). Source quality: SEO blog, benchmark unverified.

[82] cx_Freeze 8.5.0 — bdist_msi documentation
<https://cx-freeze.readthedocs.io/en/stable/bdist_msi.html>
Data: Built-in MSI creation via `bdist_msi` command.

[83] 82phil.github.io — "Package a Python Application with MSIX"

[84] GitHub — "niess/python-appimage"
<https://github.com/niess/python-appimage>
Data: Relocatable Python as AppImage; weekly updates; recipe-based app building.

[85] pyproject-appimage — PyPI
<https://pypi.org/project/pyproject-appimage/>
Data: Simpler AppImage building via pyproject.toml configuration.

[86] Flathub Discourse — "How can I package a python tkinter application?"
<https://discourse.flathub.org/t/how-can-i-package-a-python-tkinter-application/3795>
Data: tkinter not pip-installable; must come from Flatpak SDK.

[87] Flatpak Documentation — "Sandbox Permissions"
<https://docs.flatpak.org/en/latest/sandbox-permissions.html>
Data: `--socket=x11`, `--socket=wayland`, `--socket=fallback-x11`, `--share=ipc`.

[88] Snapcraft Forum — "Tkinter doesn't work with the python plugin"
<https://forum.snapcraft.io/t/tkinter-doesnt-work-with-the-python-plugin/16567>
Data: `python3-tk` must be added as `stage-package` in snapcraft.yaml.

[89] Debian Packages — "python3-tk in sid"
<https://packages.debian.org/sid/python3-tk>
Data: tkinter is a separate package from python3 on Debian/Ubuntu.

[90] Freedesktop — "Desktop Entry Specification"
<https://specifications.freedesktop.org/desktop-entry-spec/>
Data: .desktop file format for Linux menu integration.

[91] ArchWiki — "Desktop entries"
<https://wiki.archlinux.org/title/Desktop_entries>
Data: Installation locations, required fields, categories.

[92] BeeWare Briefcase Issue #318
<https://github.com/beeware/briefcase/issues/318>
Data: Briefcase does not include tkinter on Windows (embeddable Python limitation).

[93] fbs Official Site
<https://build-system.fman.io/>
Data: Qt-only; does not support tkinter.

[94] PEP 508 — "Dependency specification"
<https://peps.python.org/pep-0508/>
Data: Environment markers for platform-conditional dependencies.

[95] GitHub — "dennisvang/tufup"
<https://github.com/dennisvang/tufup>
Data: TUF-based auto-updater for standalone Python apps. Packaging-tool-agnostic.

[96] Data-Dive — "Build a multi OS Python app: PyInstaller on GitHub Actions"

Data: Matrix strategy for multi-OS builds with artifact upload.
